findings.problem: Once the committee has effectively chosen deferral, the back half of the scene keeps restating provenance, timing, and contribution in near-synonymous loops. The result is procedural relitigation instead of escalating dramatic pressure, so the chapter spends tension after the outcome is already legible.
findings.rewrite_direction: Compress or merge one of the repeated objection loops centered on snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:177, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:193, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:215, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:239, then use the recovered space before snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:251 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:261 for a distinct irreversible beat: a concrete procedural consequence, a sharper faculty self-protection reveal, or one last tactical move from Leela that fails in a new way rather than another restatement of the same no.
findings.acceptance_test: Reading snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:177-205, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:215-239, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:251-261, each exchange cluster must introduce a new pressure or loss of option. If any one cluster can be removed without losing a distinct beat, the revision fails.

findings.problem: The narration repeatedly explains Mohit's rhetorical move immediately after he speaks. Those glosses are accurate, but the accumulation starts doing the reader's inferential work and weakens the scene's confidence in its own dramatization.
findings.rewrite_direction: In the explanatory follow-up paragraphs at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:145, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:157, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:205, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:239, keep only the sharpest one or two interpretive judgments. Replace the others with observable consequences in the room: Leela's involuntary body response, a committee member's note-taking or silence, or a procedural shift that lets the reader feel Mohit's tactic without being told how to parse it every time.
findings.acceptance_test: Across the four cited follow-up beats, no more than two may explicitly name Mohit's tactic or summarize what he has just done. At least two must instead land through concrete reaction or room behavior that lets the coercive effect be inferred by reading the passage.

findings.problem: Mohit, Reyes, Weiss, and Gloria repeatedly settle into the same polished committee diction around "the issue," "the record," "timing," and "clarity." That shared abstract register weakens idiolect separation and makes the faculty side of the scene sound more authored than overheard.
findings.rewrite_direction: Differentiate the faculty voices in the committee exchanges anchored at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:71, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:85, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:205, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:251, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:255, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:261, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:279. Keep Mohit soft, managerial, and selectively warm; keep Gloria shorter and more process-clipped; let Reyes sound substantively exact rather than administratively vague; let Weiss sound skeptical and clause-heavy instead of echoing the same abstractions.
findings.acceptance_test: If speaker tags are removed from the revised committee dialogue in snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:195-205, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:251-261, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:275-279, a careful reader should still be able to distinguish Mohit, Gloria, Reyes, and Weiss by diction and syntax alone. The exact frame "the issue" may appear at most once in the revised committee dialogue.

findings.problem: Two sentence defaults become visible tics over the length of the chapter: repeated "as if" comparison clauses and repeated use of "the room" as an acting subject. Both are individually serviceable, but at this density they start to feel reusable rather than freshly discovered.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise the cited spans at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:17, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:29, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:97, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:145, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:171, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:247, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:257, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:289, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:23, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:65, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:121, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:163, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:197, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:249, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:295 so that most of the pressure is carried by direct image, concrete object, or specific human action rather than by "as if" scaffolds or by making "the room" do the acting.
findings.acceptance_test: In the cited spans, no more than two "as if" comparisons may remain and no more than two sentences may use "the room" as the acting subject. Each replacement must name a concrete agent, object, or bodily action that the reader can point to on the page.

summary: Past tense, continuity, and the required bodily fallout all hold, and no canon contradiction or sanitization failure is evident from the allowed inputs. The chapter still falls short of pass level because the committee scene over-repeats its procedural refusal, the narration too often decodes Mohit's rhetoric for the reader, faculty dialogue converges into one institutional register, and recurring "as if"/"the room" scaffolds become visible prose habits; style-bible voice auditing had to rely on the book-wide dialogue rules plus continuity-sheet register notes because outline/style_bible.json does not supply per-character dialogue metadata.
